extra.reasoning: The mutual-exclusion property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is violated. rec_drug_a is set true only by r2 (priority 50): IF (elderly AND pregnancy AND low_ejection_fraction). rec_drug_b is set true only by r3 (priority 50): IF elderly. When elderly=true, pregnancy=true, low_ejection_fraction=true, the antecedent of r2 holds (so rec_drug_a := true) and the antecedent of r3 holds (so rec_drug_b := true). No competing rule sets rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b to false; the defaults (false) only apply to outputs left unset, and here both are set true by their respective rules. Priority/tie-breaking is irrelevant because no other rule targets rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b. Therefore both outputs are simultaneously true, violating the property. The remaining inputs (renal_impairment, bradycardia, diabetes) only affect rec_drug_c and are set false in the witness; their values do not change the outcome for a and b. This input state is clearly reachable (no constraints on the input space). Counterexample: elderly=true, pregnancy=true, low_ejection_fraction=true, renal_impairment=false, bradycardia=false, diabetes=false yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true.
